assumptions.statement: Core, deterministic conformance and required adapter baselines run without a live surface; live cost calibration and live adapter promotion are separate.
assumptions.impact: Required adapters remain EXPERIMENTAL until optional live promotion, while production release still requires the bounded provider-neutral cost calibration.

assumptions.statement: Offline release-candidate generation is autonomous. Production promotion later supplies signed unexpired neutrality, CI matrix, benchmark-baseline and live-calibration authorities from outside the repository.
assumptions.impact: The standalone candidate can complete locally, while any production/verified-external claim remains blocked until those authorities are present and verified.

decisions.decision: Use SDD tier S2 and require independent specification, plan, task and final reviews.
decisions.rationale: The release spans security, architecture, resources, packaging and multiple host surfaces.

decisions.decision: Implement lifecycle semantics once in the Python core; keep skills thin and adapters protocol-only.
decisions.rationale: A single semantic source prevents cross-surface drift.

decisions.decision: Require explicit execution approval after freeze and ask before remediation.
decisions.rationale: Planning may complete autonomously without silently starting repository-wide implementation.

decisions.decision: Close rejected plan-review findings with executable controller gates, zero-open-Medium acceptance, offline contract inventories, versioned resource authorities and authenticated external release evidence.
decisions.rationale: Revision 2 must convert prose assurances into content-addressed, independently auditable contracts.

decisions.decision: Run WS-01 bootstrap neutrality through python -m agent_lifecycle.neutrality and reserve python -m agent_lifecycle for WS-07 and later tasks.
decisions.rationale: WS-01 owns the neutrality module but not the package-level __main__.py entry point owned by WS-07; the module command preserves exact write ownership without changing product behavior.

decisions.decision: Use neutrality subject projection v2 with operation-bound write-once outputs, content-bound primary artifacts and a detached signed commit receipt.
decisions.rationale: Absent-at-scan and create-no-replace publication plus read-only identical replay close output-path, substitution, partial-publication and self-receipt fixpoints without weakening complete subject coverage.

decisions.decision: Keep WS-14 sequential and publish one local content-addressed offline release-candidate generation; do not claim production promotion without later external authorities.
decisions.rationale: A local deterministic candidate is auditable and portable, while production promotion requires signed platform, neutrality and live-cost evidence outside the repository.

decisions.decision: Treat EV-FINAL as READY_FOR_FINALIZATION, enter AWAITING_FINAL_PROOF after accepted terminal review, run finalization neutrality, and reserve COMPLETE for the acyclic controller CAS/WAL transaction.
decisions.rationale: The terminal review becomes part of the final scanned input while fixed operation paths, separate body and envelope digests, idempotent WAL append and state CAS close the sequence without a recursive post-proof gate.

decisions.decision: Use one frozen AGENT_LIFECYCLE_NEUTRALITY_* external authority environment contract for bootstrap, runtime, finalization and release.
decisions.rationale: Bounded fresh signed regular files outside workspace, Git, artifact and release roots prevent repository-controlled authority, env drift and secret leakage.

decisions.decision: Distinguish candidatePayloadInventoryDigest from finalInventoryDigest throughout matrix, assembly, neutrality and verification.
decisions.rationale: The platform matrix can bind reproducible payload bytes without referring to a future generation that later includes aggregate and provenance identities.

decisions.decision: Refreeze WS-14 as an offline release-candidate and defer production promotion to a separate external-authority gate.
decisions.rationale: The standalone repository must be autonomous and cannot require unavailable project-specific CI, signing or live model receipts to finish the local lifecycle.

leadOwned.reason: Mutable workflow state, packet publication and operation artifacts belong to the controller.
Controller-owned refreeze support for preserving compatible accepted tasks across plan adoption.
Controller-owned CLI flag for compatible accepted-task preservation during refreeze adoption.
Controller-owned regression coverage for compatible accepted-task preservation during refreeze adoption.
Controller-owned public workflow export for finalization.
Controller-owned next-action projection for FINAL_AUDIT finalization.

workstreams.title: Bootstrap package and neutrality boundary
workstreams.launchGate: LG-01 frozen plan verified and controller-validation receipt authority supplied by the controller runtime

plannedItems.description: Before downstream extraction, fixture, skill, adapter or release work starts, WS-01 shall pass an autonomous local bootstrap boundary: the controller-managed generic repository-policy scan, the complete neutrality negative suite and the foundation suite shall run with Python stdlib only, without pytest, network, host-operation approval or committed origin-specific deny values. Every local validation command shall consume the signed controller operation request, write only declared controller protocol outputs, bind run, package, task, attempt, phase, operation, command, plan digest, source revision and canonical path, and fail closed on any non-zero neutrality counter, output conflict, skipped input, opaque input, read race, incomplete scan, unsupported archive, archive-limit breach or path alias conflict. Origin-specific deny values and Ed25519 signing material shall remain outside the standalone repository and shall be supplied only to downstream pre-launch, post-attempt, release and finalization neutrality gates through the bounded fresh external NEUTRALITY_* authority contract.

The kit shall provide a reproducible Python 3.11 through 3.13 package foundation with one locked dependency graph, Apache-2.0 notices, stable versioning, clean-environment installation metadata, and a single canonical implementation source; cross-platform release proof is governed separately by REQ-PLATFORM-MATRIX.

workstreams.title: Public contracts, schemas and host protocol
workstreams.launchGate: LG-02 WS-01 accepted with bootstrap and negative-suite receipts, then the controller pre-launch gate passed

plannedItems.description: The core shall expose versioned JSON-compatible contracts, JSON Schemas, stable error codes, normalized repository-relative paths, a provider-neutral host-operation protocol, and a pluggable change-set boundary without embedding host or model selection logic.

workstreams.title: Specification, planning, review and freeze services
workstreams.launchGate: LG-03 WS-02 accepted and the controller pre-launch gate passed

plannedItems.description: The core shall implement SDD tiers S0, S1, and S2 with deterministic clarification, specification validation, independent specification review, plan validation, independent plan review, immutable freeze locks, revision lineage, reopened state, and fail-closed refreeze rules.

workstreams.title: Frozen DAG task compiler
workstreams.launchGate: LG-04 WS-02 accepted and the controller pre-launch gate passed

plannedItems.description: The compiler shall transform only a frozen, hash-verified plan DAG into deterministic task packets containing task-local projections, exact write ownership, launch gates, capability requirements, validation contracts, reviewer routes, and bounded context references.

workstreams.title: Durable workflow, usage and resource control
workstreams.launchGate: LG-05 WS-02 accepted and the controller pre-launch gate passed

plannedItems.description: The workflow engine shall durably enforce dependency waves, approval modes, actor and reviewer independence, task attempts, per-step review, remediation, contract-change reopening, cancellation, deadlines, atomic state transitions, append-only events, crash recovery, resume and reconciliation. Terminal completion shall be finite: the independent final audit reconciles all predecessors and the terminal pre-launch gate; the terminal post-attempt gate covers that audit result; an independent review accepts the terminal attempt; the controller enters AWAITING_FINAL_PROOF bound to exact state and WAL predecessors; finalization neutrality covers the accepted review and all existing artifacts; and only a closed acyclic proof envelope at a fixed run/operation write-once path, followed by one idempotent fsynced WAL append and expected-revision atomic state replacement, may transition to COMPLETE. Identical replay shall return the same proof and state, while any conflicting replay, crash-recovery mismatch or plan, source, authority, review, gate, inventory or change-set drift fails closed.

The host protocol shall record an end-to-end usage ledger for clarification, specification authoring and review, planning and review, implementation, task review, remediation, and final audit, preserving raw provider counters plus host-attested normalized billable tokens, prompt identities, cache usage, context bytes, tool calls, validation runs, and wall time.

The lifecycle shall enforce the immutable machine-readable agent-lifecycle-budget-profile.v1 product profile: explicit hard logical-semantic-call ceilings per task and per run; context-byte ceilings per call and cumulatively per run; tier-wide hard ceilings for billable tokens, tool calls, validations, attempts, evidence bytes, parallelism, and wall time; stage-specific hard billable-token ceilings; and mandatory absolute release SLOs. Each semantic iteration shall permit zero or one logical semantic call; task and run counters shall remain cumulative across attempts, stages, review, remediation, cache use, and fallback; optional diagnostics shall never weaken a hard gate; and any hard-ceiling or unverified usage-attestation breach shall fail closed.

The lifecycle shall map clarification, specification authoring, specification review, plan authoring, plan review, implementation, task review, remediation, and final audit to provider-neutral capability profiles in external host configuration; profiles may resolve to one or different host models, but selection and ordered fallback shall be deterministic, fallback shall preserve every mandatory capability and independence class, unavailable mandatory capability shall fail closed, and every usage receipt shall record the stage, role, selected profile id, configuration digest, opaque host model identity, fallback decision, and attributed usage without embedding provider or model names in core contracts.

The controller shall execute the frozen neutrality v3 gate before every downstream launch and after every task attempt with an operation-unique create-no-replace detached receipt, exact output-descriptor and primary-artifact manifests, producer/verifier recomputation of output, archive, external-authority, worktree, Git-object, scope, subject and claims digests, fresh verified NEUTRALITY_* authority, and all required zero counters. Any occupancy, replay mismatch, authority drift, alias, archive breach, incomplete scan or read race shall durably block launch or acceptance; rule values, trust material, private keys, matched fragments and credentials shall never enter packets, state, logs, prompts or durable evidence. After the accepted terminal review, the controller shall additionally run the frozen finalization-neutrality projection over that review and every prior artifact before deterministic proof, WAL and state outputs may be committed.

The workflow controller shall move a task to ACCEPTED only after an independent findings-first task audit verifies the frozen packet, exact write ownership, result, evidence and usage lineage and reports no unresolved MEDIUM, HIGH, or BLOCKER finding; a BLOCKER shall stop execution immediately, while any other rejected verdict shall follow the frozen remediation or contract-change route without silent repair.

workstreams.title: Independent audit and change sets
workstreams.launchGate: LG-06 WS-02 accepted and the controller pre-launch gate passed

plannedItems.description: The audit service shall independently verify each task and the final implementation against the frozen specification, plan, exact write sets, change set, evidence receipts, acceptance criteria, and immutable lock, returning machine-readable findings-first verdicts without silently repairing implementation.

workstreams.title: Stable compact JSON CLI
workstreams.launchGate: LG-07 WS-03 through WS-06 accepted and the controller pre-launch gate passed

plannedItems.description: The package shall provide a stable compact JSON CLI for specification, plan, task compilation, workflow, audit, adapter, conformance, neutrality, and schema operations, with full state available only by explicit request, no host-specific shell wrappers in core, and a thin dispatcher over modular controller services rather than production lifecycle semantics inside one monolithic script.

The workflow controller shall be implemented as a modular Python package with a thin CLI facade. Production lifecycle semantics shall not live in one large workflowctl.py-style script. The implementation shall keep one-way dependencies from CLI to API to use-case modules to contracts/ports, keep adapters outside core imports, and pass small-context size gates for source files, functions, dispatchers and launchers. SOLID boundaries are required for state transition, artifact store, operation publication, host adapter, usage attestation, authorization gate and validator execution; DRY is required for canonical JSON, digest, WAL, publication, receipt, path and capability logic; YAGNI forbids hosted control planes, mandatory MCP/app servers, provider-specific routing in core, copied project samples and live adapter promotion as a release prerequisite.

workstreams.title: Five canonical thin lifecycle skills
workstreams.launchGate: LG-08 WS-07 accepted and the controller pre-launch gate passed

plannedItems.description: The distribution shall contain exactly five canonical thin lifecycle skills for plan authoring, independent plan audit, task packet compilation, durable orchestration, and independent implementation audit; each entry shall remain stage-routed, project-neutral, surface-neutral, and no larger than 8192 bytes.

workstreams.title: Synthetic conformance and cost benchmarks
workstreams.launchGate: LG-09 WS-07 accepted and the controller pre-launch gate passed

plannedItems.description: The kit shall include only synthetic, inventoried fixtures and deterministic conformance scenarios covering S0, S1, and S2 happy paths, repeated review, stale or forged artifacts, ownership conflicts, cyclic DAGs, reopen and refreeze, parallel execution, rejection and remediation, crash recovery, budget exhaustion, path attacks, adapter failure, and final verdicts across supported operating systems.

Every required surface-adapter workstream for Codex, Claude Code, Cursor, Hermes and OpenCode shall be fully executable offline from the frozen normalized contract inventory and shall produce an EXPERIMENTAL adapter that installs and discovers deterministically, validates every versioned protocol envelope, declares capabilities and maturity, pins a closed offline contract-compatibility range plus content-addressed authoritative source provenance, omits any live-tested host range, fails closed for unsupported operations, and passes the shared synthetic baseline. Live surface runtime is never a required release prerequisite; VERIFIED is an optional later promotion requiring a bounded live-tested host range and fresh live evidence for install, discovery, launch, wait, cancel, resume, tool execution, result collection, usage attestation, task audit, and final audit.

The release accounting-conformance suite shall use deterministic synthetic-host replay with scenario-specific replayed semantic calls and zero live model invocations to measure the frozen S0, S1, and S2 corpus, schema-valid normalized usage attestations, separate cold and warm cache cohorts, at least twenty runs per scenario and cohort, zero quality or audit regression, no hard-ceiling violation, and mandatory absolute p95 targets for billable tokens, cumulative context bytes per run, tool calls, and wall time; the first release shall publish the exact independently audited baseline through the frozen Ed25519 baseline authority, and every later release shall additionally remain within five percent p50 and ten percent p95 of the immediately prior verified signed baseline.

workstreams.title: Codex surface adapter
workstreams.launchGate: LG-10 WS-08 and WS-09 accepted, frozen offline contract input verified and the controller pre-launch gate passed

plannedItems.description: The Codex adapter shall satisfy the shared offline adapter baseline, publish a bounded compatibility descriptor and evidence-accurate EXPERIMENTAL maturity without requiring a live surface runtime, map every available host operation without reimplementing core semantics, and fail closed for absent capabilities; only the separate optional live promotion route may mark it VERIFIED.

workstreams.title: Claude surface adapter
workstreams.launchGate: LG-11 WS-08 and WS-09 accepted, frozen offline contract input verified and the controller pre-launch gate passed

plannedItems.description: The Claude adapter shall satisfy the shared offline adapter baseline, publish a bounded compatibility descriptor and evidence-accurate EXPERIMENTAL maturity without requiring a live surface runtime, map every available host operation without reimplementing core semantics, and fail closed for absent capabilities; only the separate optional live promotion route may mark it VERIFIED.

workstreams.title: Cursor surface adapter
workstreams.launchGate: LG-12 WS-08 and WS-09 accepted, frozen offline contract input verified and the controller pre-launch gate passed

plannedItems.description: The Cursor adapter shall satisfy the shared offline adapter baseline, publish a bounded compatibility descriptor and evidence-accurate EXPERIMENTAL maturity without requiring a live surface runtime, map every available host operation without reimplementing core semantics, and fail closed for absent capabilities; only the separate optional live promotion route may mark it VERIFIED.

workstreams.title: OpenCode surface adapter
workstreams.launchGate: LG-13 WS-08 and WS-09 accepted, frozen offline contract input verified and the controller pre-launch gate passed

plannedItems.description: The OpenCode adapter shall satisfy the shared offline adapter baseline, publish a bounded compatibility descriptor and evidence-accurate EXPERIMENTAL maturity without requiring a live surface runtime, map every available host operation without reimplementing core semantics, and fail closed for absent capabilities; only the separate optional live promotion route may mark it VERIFIED.

workstreams.title: Hermes surface adapter
workstreams.launchGate: LG-16 WS-08 and WS-09 accepted, frozen offline contract input verified and the controller pre-launch gate passed

plannedItems.description: The Hermes adapter shall satisfy the shared offline adapter baseline, publish a bounded compatibility descriptor and evidence-accurate EXPERIMENTAL maturity without requiring a live Hermes runtime, project the shared lifecycle skills into Hermes skill-directory discovery, support slash-command invocation where available, map every available host operation without reimplementing core semantics, and fail closed for absent capabilities; only the separate optional live promotion route may mark it VERIFIED.

workstreams.title: Governance and release engineering
workstreams.launchGate: LG-14 WS-08 through WS-13 and WS-16 accepted, r13 finalization-capable source tree present, and the local controller pre-launch gate passed; no external release authority is required for the offline candidate

plannedItems.description: The offline release-candidate shall assemble a content-addressed local release payload from the current source tree, shared skills, adapters, conformance corpus, documentation and governance files; verify two deterministic inventory passes over the same payload bytes; publish no production-ready or verified-external claim; and leave production promotion blocked until signed external release authorities exist.

Offline release-candidate readiness shall run local current-tree and release-payload neutrality scans with zero findings under the portable policy. Signed all-refs, reflog, unreachable-object and all-archive release neutrality remains a deferred production-promotion gate and shall not be claimed by the offline candidate.

The kit shall publish CI workflow and support-matrix contracts for Ubuntu, macOS and Windows on CPython 3.11, 3.12 and 3.13 as production-promotion requirements. The offline candidate validates the matrix contract and adapter EXPERIMENTAL maturity locally without claiming authenticated matrix leg receipts.

The kit shall publish a provider-neutral live cost calibration contract as a deferred production-promotion gate. The offline candidate validates the deterministic synthetic baseline and documents the required external environment and receipt inputs without claiming live model execution.

workstreams.title: Independent integrated final audit
workstreams.launchGate: LG-15 WS-14 accepted, every required task independently accepted and the controller pre-launch gate passed

plannedItems.description: The terminal independent findings-first audit shall reconcile accepted tasks, write sets, local release-candidate inventory, local neutrality, conformance, adapter maturity, controller state, finalization capability and deferred production-promotion blockers against the immutable lock. It may report READY_FOR_FINALIZATION for the offline candidate only when no production-ready or verified-external claim is emitted and no unresolved MEDIUM, HIGH or BLOCKER finding remains. The controller shall then publish one canonical agent-run-final-proof.v1 artifact and transition the run to COMPLETE with productionPromotionClaimed false.

workers.launchGate: LG-01 frozen plan verified and external bootstrap-neutrality authority supplied by the host
LG-02 WS-01 accepted with bootstrap and negative-suite receipts, then the controller pre-launch gate passed
LG-03 WS-02 accepted and the controller pre-launch gate passed
LG-04 WS-02 accepted and the controller pre-launch gate passed
LG-05 WS-02 accepted and the controller pre-launch gate passed
LG-06 WS-02 accepted and the controller pre-launch gate passed
LG-07 WS-03 through WS-06 accepted and the controller pre-launch gate passed
LG-08 WS-07 accepted and the controller pre-launch gate passed
LG-09 WS-07 accepted and the controller pre-launch gate passed
LG-10 WS-08 and WS-09 accepted, frozen offline contract input verified and the controller pre-launch gate passed
LG-11 WS-08 and WS-09 accepted, frozen offline contract input verified and the controller pre-launch gate passed
LG-12 WS-08 and WS-09 accepted, frozen offline contract input verified and the controller pre-launch gate passed
LG-13 WS-08 and WS-09 accepted, frozen offline contract input verified and the controller pre-launch gate passed
LG-16 WS-08 and WS-09 accepted, frozen offline contract input verified and the controller pre-launch gate passed
LG-14 WS-08 through WS-13 and WS-16 accepted, external CI, baseline and live-calibration authorities approved, and the controller pre-launch gate passed
LG-15 WS-14 accepted, every required task independently accepted and the controller pre-launch gate passed

evidenceArtifacts.description: Controller-managed local generic neutrality scan report and receipt for REQ-NEUTRALITY bootstrap autonomy
Controller-managed stdlib negative-suite report and receipt for REQ-NEUTRALITY
Controller-managed stdlib package foundation report and receipt for REQ-FOUNDATION
Fresh evidence for REQ-RESOURCE covering explicit task/run logical-call and per-call/cumulative-run context ceilings
Authenticated nine-leg aggregate bound to one candidate payload digest for REQ-PLATFORM-MATRIX
Atomic committed release generation evidence for REQ-RELEASE
Independent READY_FOR_FINALIZATION evidence for REQ-FINAL; only the later finalization-neutrality and controller transaction may issue COMPLETE
